assertions.text: Output contains a markdown table (using | column | syntax)
assertions.evidence: The output contains a markdown table starting on line 3 with pipe-delimited columns.

assertions.text: At least one output column name ends with '?' (e.g. 'Eligible?' or 'Approved?')
assertions.evidence: Column names include 'Approved?' and 'Rejection Reason?' — both end with '?'.

assertions.text: Column names use domain/business language — no implementation terms like 'creditScore', 'isEligible', 'boolean', 'int'
assertions.evidence: Column names are: Scenario, Credit Score, Credit Score Threshold (Policy), Applicant Age, Income Status, Approved?, Rejection Reason? — all plain business language with no camelCase or type names.

assertions.text: Table includes at least one row specifically for the senior applicant (65+) threshold scenario
assertions.evidence: Multiple senior rows are present, including 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold' (age 65, score 600) and 'Senior applicant, above senior threshold' (age 65, score 620).

assertions.text: The missing income scenario is represented — either as a row with an open/uncertain expected value (?, TBD, or blank) or as an explicit open question note
assertions.evidence: Rows 'Standard applicant, income info missing' and 'Senior applicant, income info missing' both have a blank Income Status and Approved? = '?', plus an explicit open questions section addressing this scenario.

assertions.text: Scenario names describe conditions ('Senior applicant at threshold') not outcomes ('Approved')
assertions.evidence: All scenario names describe conditions, e.g. 'Standard applicant, above threshold', 'Senior applicant, at senior threshold', 'Just below senior age, standard threshold used' — none name outcomes like 'Approved' or 'Rejected'.
